content.json:
# TCP — Trading Central Panel · Operations dashboard

**Audience**: on-call operator (single user during the academic phase) and reviewer.  
**Refresh**: manual — click the refresh button in the toolbar after changing the **Time range** parameter (academic-phase posture per obs-MI-07; auto-refresh deferred to Etapa-12 polish).  
**SLOs**: see `docs/observability/slo.md` for the formal SLI/SLO definitions and burn-rate alerts.

Queries are mirrored in `infra/observability/kusto/*.kql` (single source of truth). When you tweak a query in the portal, **mirror the change back into the .kql file in the same PR** — the workbook is `isLocked: true` to prevent accidental drift.

---

## SLI-1 — `/api/ask` assistant latency

p95 latency target: **≤ 3 000 ms** (warm requests). The alert `tcp-alert-ask-p95-latency` fires when p95 exceeds **4 000 ms** for 15 minutes.

content.query:
requests
| where operation_Name == "ask"
| where success == true
| where timestamp {TimeRange}
| summarize p50_ms=percentile(duration, 50), p95_ms=percentile(duration, 95), p99_ms=percentile(duration, 99), samples=count() by bin(timestamp, 1h)
| where samples > 5
| order by timestamp asc
| render timechart
content.title: Assistant latency p50 / p95 / p99 (successful warm requests only)

content.query:
requests
| where timestamp {TimeRange}
| summarize total=count(), failed=countif(success == false) by operation_Name
| extend error_rate_pct = round(100.0 * failed / iif(total == 0, 1, total), 2)
| order by error_rate_pct desc
content.title: Error rate by operation

content.json:
## SLI-2 — Daily generator outcomes

Target: **≥ 99 % success** over a rolling 30-day window. Alert `tcp-alert-daily-generator-failed` fires on any single failure within the previous 24 hours.

content.query:
requests
| where operation_Name == "daily_generator"
| where timestamp {TimeRange}
| extend day_ro = bin(timestamp, 1d)
| summarize Runs=count(), Successes=countif(success == true), Failures=countif(success == false) by day_ro
| extend success_rate_pct = round(100.0 * Successes / iif(Runs == 0, 1, Runs), 2)
| order by day_ro asc
content.title: Daily generator runs vs failures

content.json:
## Anthropic token usage and EUR cost projection

Budget: see `docs/observability/slo.md` § cost guardrails. The alert `tcp-alert-anthropic-cost-burn` fires when the rolling 7-day spend projects above **€10/month**.

content.title: Anthropic tokens + EUR estimate

content.json:
## Function App cold starts

Indicator for the AskAssistant cold-path latency. Sustained cold-start spikes during business hours (Mon–Fri 09:00–18:00 RO) suggest the warmup timer is not firing as expected.

content.query:
traces
| where timestamp {TimeRange}
| where customDimensions["Category"] == "Host.Startup"
| where message has "Worker process started"
| extend startup_ms = todouble(customDimensions["StartupDurationMs"])
| extend bucket = case(isnull(startup_ms), "unknown", startup_ms <= 1000, "warm", startup_ms <= 2000, "lukewarm", "cold")
| summarize cold=countif(bucket == "cold"), lukewarm=countif(bucket == "lukewarm"), warm=countif(bucket == "warm"), unknown=countif(bucket == "unknown") by bin(timestamp, 1h)
| order by timestamp asc
| render timechart
content.title: Cold vs warm worker starts

content.json:
## Azure SQL Free-Offer vCore-second budget

Free grant: **100 000 vCore-seconds / month** (resets first day of the calendar month UTC). Alert `tcp-alert-sql-quota-burn` fires at 80 % consumption.

content.query:
let monthly_quota = 100000.0;
AzureMetrics
| where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.SQL"
| where MetricName == "app_cpu_billed"
| where TimeGenerated > startofmonth(now())
| summarize used = sum(Total) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), Resource
| partition by Resource (sort by TimeGenerated asc | extend cumulative = row_cumsum(used))
| extend pct_used = round(100.0 * cumulative / monthly_quota, 2)
| project TimeGenerated, Resource, used, cumulative, pct_used
content.title: SQL vCore-second consumption (month-to-date)

content.json:
## BACPAC weekly export health (ADR-004)

Every Sunday 08:00 RO. Alert `tcp-alert-bacpac-missed` fires when no `tcp.bacpac.complete` event lands within the previous 8 days.

content.query:
traces
| where timestamp > ago(30d)
| where customDimensions["event"] in ("tcp.bacpac.complete", "tcp.bacpac.failed", "tcp.bacpac.skipped")
| extend duration_ms = tolong(customDimensions["duration_ms"]), size_bytes = tolong(customDimensions["size_bytes"]), status = tostring(customDimensions["status"])
| summarize runs=count(), successes=countif(status == "succeeded"), skipped=countif(status == "already_exists"), failures=countif(status in ("failed", "Failed", "Canceled")), avg_duration_ms=avg(duration_ms), avg_size_mb=round(avg(size_bytes)/1024.0/1024.0, 2) by bin(timestamp, 7d)
| order by timestamp desc
content.title: BACPAC weekly health

content.json:
## Rate-limit refusals (`/api/ask` 429 returns)

Alert `tcp-alert-rate-limit-spike` fires when refusals exceed **50/h**.

content.query:
traces
| where timestamp {TimeRange}
| where message has "tcp.func.ask.rate_limited"
| extend oid_suffix = tostring(customDimensions["oid_suffix"])
| summarize refusals=count(), distinct_oid_suffixes=dcount(oid_suffix) by bin(timestamp, 1h)
| order by timestamp asc
| render timechart
content.title: Rate-limit refusals

content.json:
## Question fingerprints (PII-redacted)

SHA-256 hashes only — the original prompt text is **never** persisted (see `docs/security/threat_model.md` S05 + the `tests/integration/test_telemetry_no_pii.py` enforcement test). Use this list to spot abusive repetition or to verify the audit hook is wired.

content.query:
traces
| where timestamp > ago(30d)
| where customDimensions["event"] == "tcp.ask.audit"
| extend q_hash = tostring(customDimensions["question_sha256"])
| where isnotempty(q_hash)
| summarize last_seen=max(timestamp), occurrences=count() by q_hash
| top 50 by last_seen desc
content.title: Last 50 distinct question fingerprints
